title: Next Greater Element I
description: The next greater element of some element x in an array is the first greater element that is to the right of x in the same array. You are given two distinct 0-indexed integer arrays nums1 and nums2, where nums1 is a subset of nums2. For each 0 <= i < nums1.length, find the index j such that nums1[i] == nums2[j] and determine the next greater element of nums2[j] in nums2. If there is no next greater element, then the answer for this query is -1. Return an array ans of length nums1.length such that ans[i] is the next greater element as described above.
hints: Use a monotonic decreasing stack to precompute the next greater element for each element in nums2.
Store results in a hash map for O(1) lookups.

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given an array of integers temperatures represents the daily temperatures, return an array answer such that answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait after the ith day to get a warmer temperature. If there is no future day for which this is possible, keep answer[i] == 0 instead.
hints: Use a monotonic decreasing stack storing indices.
When a warmer temperature is found, pop from the stack and calculate the difference in indices.

title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description: Given an array of integers heights representing the histogram's bar height where the width of each bar is 1, return the area of the largest rectangle in the histogram.
hints: Use a monotonic increasing stack to track indices of bars.
When a shorter bar is encountered, calculate areas for all taller bars that end at the current index.

title: Sum of Subarray Minimums
description: Given an array of integers arr, find the sum of min(b), over every contiguous subarray b of arr. Since the answer may be large, return the answer modulo 10^9 + 7.
hints: For each element, find how many subarrays it is the minimum of.
Use a monotonic increasing stack with previous less element (PLE) and next less element (NLE) indices.

title: Remove K Digits
description: Given string num representing a non-negative integer num, and an integer k, return the smallest possible integer after removing k digits from num.
hints: Use a monotonic increasing stack to greedily remove larger digits.
Remove digits from the left when a smaller digit is found.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time. Implement the MinStack class: void push(int val) pushes the element val onto the stack, void pop() removes the element on the top of the stack, int top() gets the top element of the stack, int getMin() retrieves the minimum element in the stack. You must implement a solution with O(1) time complexity for each function.
hints: Use two stacks: one for the actual values and one to track the current minimum.
The min stack stores the minimum value at each level of the stack.

title: Remove Duplicate Letters
description: Given a string s, remove duplicate letters so that every letter appears once and only once. You must make sure your result is the smallest in lexicographical order among all possible results.
hints: Use a monotonic increasing stack to build the result.
Track remaining occurrences of each character to know if a character can be removed.

title: Maximal Rectangle
description: Given a rows x cols binary matrix filled with 0's and 1's, find the largest rectangle containing only 1's and return its area.
hints: Convert each row into a histogram using the concept from 'Largest Rectangle in Histogram'.
For each row, update heights: if matrix[i][j] == '1', heights[j] += 1, else heights[j] = 0.

title: Next Greater Element II
description: Given a circular integer array nums (i.e., the next element of nums[nums.length - 1] is nums[0]), return the next greater number for every element in nums. The next greater number of a number x is the first greater number to its traversing-order next in the array, which means you could search circularly to find its next greater number. If it doesn't exist, return -1 for this number.
hints: Traverse the array twice to handle the circular nature.
Use a monotonic decreasing stack storing indices.

title: Subarray Minimums
description: Given an array of integers arr, find the sum of min(b), over every contiguous subarray b of arr. Since the answer may be large, return the answer modulo 10^9 + 7. This is the same as Sum of Subarray Minimums - included here for practice.
hints: Think about how many subarrays each element is the minimum of.
Use a monotonic stack to compute previous less and next less or equal indices.
Each element contributes arr[i] * count where count is the number of subarrays where it is the minimum.
